compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Analysis + fix in PR #51 (auto-merge enabled). WHICH CRITERIA should have caught it: E5 (testing, runs on story+task) and E6 (ac-text-quality, task-only) were best-positioned. E5 missed because 'thin-but-present coverage = PASS' had no concept of a mock that bypasses the new path; E6 missed because it treated the offline proving command as sufficient AND is routed task-only (never ran on the B5 STORY — so E5 is the actual catch for story cutovers). FIX (strengthen E5+E6 per direction): E5 gains PROXY-VALIDATION anti-pattern (defaulted path validated only via offline mock = MAJOR); E6 gains PROVING-COMMAND-EXERCISES-THE-CHANGE (cutover needs a live e2e AC; green offline + signed verdict necessary-but-not-sufficient); matching checklist bullets render into the finder prompt; B5-shaped eval recall cases + anti-over-fire good pair + offline presence test; docs/plan-review-gate.md cutover-DoD section; coaching via existing Pass-4 moves 7/9. Left E6 routing task-only to avoid broad over-fire (noted as possible follow-up). No index drift; 256 tests + eval-discipline pass. Will close on merge.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

ROOT CAUSE of the B5 premature-close incident. The plan-review gate reviewed B5 (pout-gloss-hoop) and returned PASS, but did NOT flag a verification-completeness deficiency in B5's acceptance criteria: AC3 ('Integration tests cover claim+close pass/fail through the workflow path with intact signing') was satisfiable ENTIRELY by OFFLINE tests using canned agents that never call resolve_prompt — so the risky new behavior (the workflow plan-review verify/coach steps run against a LIVE model) had NO acceptance criterion requiring it to be exercised end-to-end. B5 shipped + closed (signed completion verdict) with the live plan-review claim gate broken (INDETERMINATE: 'prompt references undefined variable plan'). The completion verifier correctly certified AC-satisfaction-as-written; the gap is that the GATE that reviews PLANS did not catch that the AC could pass without exercising the changed, risky path.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Author a story (like B5) that CUTS OVER a gate/engine to a new code path and DEFAULTS to it.
2. Write an AC that is satisfiable by OFFLINE/mocked tests only (no live / end-to-end exercise of the new path).
3. Run rebar review-plan on the story. 
4. Observe: PASS, with no finding that the AC permits validating the risky behavior without ever running it (no live-verification / end-to-end-DoD requirement).
5. The story ships; the live path is broken; nothing caught it until the path was hit by hand.

## Expected vs Actual

EXPECTED: the plan-review gate's AC-quality / definition-of-done criteria (e.g. E6 ac-text-quality, F1, the T-series acceptance criteria) flag an AC whose verification can pass WITHOUT exercising the actually-changed risky behavior — especially for a cutover/migration that defaults to a new path. The coaching should push 'add a live/end-to-end acceptance criterion for the path you are defaulting to.'

ACTUAL: PASS with no such finding. The verification-completeness gap (offline-only validation of a path that needs live validation) escaped review, enabling B5 to close green while broken.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A plan-review criterion (new or strengthened, e.g. the AC-quality / proxy-validation dimension) flags an AC that can be satisfied WITHOUT exercising the changed risky behavior — i.e. mocked/offline-only verification of a path the story makes the default.
- [ ] An eval/regression case encodes the B5-shaped scenario (a cutover defaulting to a new engine path with an offline-only AC) and asserts the gate surfaces the verification-completeness finding.
- [ ] The coaching for that finding recommends adding a live/end-to-end DoD for the defaulted path.
- [ ] Documented: a gate/engine cutover's DoD must include exercising the new path end-to-end against a live model (green offline tests + a passing completion verdict are necessary but not sufficient).
compiled_state.title: plan-review gate passed B5 without flagging that its AC allowed offline-only verification of a path that needed live validation
